reasoning: Without access to actual events up to November 2025, I base my forecast on the historical pace of U.S. regulatory actions regarding AI and semiconductors. Since 2022, the Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) has updated its export controls roughly once a year. While the January 2025 rule was comprehensive for advanced computing items and model weights, it explicitly did not implement specific controls on cloud access to AI computing — a known "loophole" that U.S. lawmakers and the Commerce Department have been signaling intent to close. A proposed rule regarding Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) was issued in early 2024; based on typical administrative timelines, a final rule or equivalent export control action would theoretically mature and become effective in mid-to-late 2025. This strongly aligns with the May–December 2025 target window. However, the sheer size of the January 2025 ruling and a possible governmental transition mean BIS may prioritize compliance enforcement rather than pushing out another major update before year-end, bringing the probability down slightly from historical base rates. Therefore, I estimate a 60% probability.
